presentation.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — The Business Engine

slides.title: What Did You Learn This Morning?
slides.body:
Let's reflect on your role play experience.

• What surprised you?
• What was difficult?
• What made the conversation feel natural?
slides.speakerNotes:
Open after the morning FNA role play.
Pair-share for 3 minutes, then invite 2–3 volunteers.
Transition: Great businesses are built from real experiences — not slides.
discussionQuestions: What surprised you in the role play?
What made the conversation feel natural?

slides.title: Great Businesses Are Built From Real Experiences
slides.body:
This morning you didn't study Client Experience.
You experienced Client Experience.

Now let's connect it to our Financial Entrepreneurship Canvas — strengthen Box 4 (Client Experience) and Box 5 (Winning Strategy).
slides.speakerNotes:
Bridge from role play to FEC.
Emphasize: BUILD TRUST · LISTEN DEEPLY · ASK BETTER QUESTIONS · CREATE IMPACT.
Point interns to the Playbook portfolio mission.
discussionQuestions: How does this morning's experience change how you think about client experience?

slides.title: Revise Your Client Experience — FEC Box 4
slides.body:
Today's mission: Improve our client experience using what we learned this morning.

Reflection prompts:
• What worked? What created impact?
• What felt awkward or difficult?
• What builds trust?
• What should our ideal client experience look like?
slides.speakerNotes:
Workshop time — interns open Playbook and Edit FEC Box 4.
Circulate squads. Probe for specific moments from role play, not generic answers.
Target: 40–45 minutes.
discussionQuestions: What should your ideal client journey look like from start to finish?

slides.title: Build Your Winning Strategy — FEC Box 5
slides.body:
Why will clients choose your advisory practice instead of another?

Compete on experience. Win with trust.
Not products. Not price. But experience, understanding, and trust.
slides.speakerNotes:
Transition to Box 5 after Box 4 drafts are underway.
Remind: strategy is why you win — not what you sell.
Interns Edit FEC Box 5 in Playbook.
discussionQuestions: Why will clients choose your practice over another advisor?

slides.title: Can Your Venture Become a Business?
slides.body:
A great client experience is the foundation.
Now let's turn it into a scalable, sustainable, and profitable business.
slides.speakerNotes:
Pivot from FEC boxes to the Business Engine.
Ask: Do you have an idea — or the start of a venture?
Preview the 10–5–3–1–₱10,000–3 model.
discussionQuestions: What makes a venture a business — not just an activity?

slides.title: The Business Engine
slides.body: 10 Prospects → 5 Discovery Conversations → 3 Solution Presentations → 1 New Client → ₱10,000 Revenue → 3 Referrals → repeat.
slides.speakerNotes:
Walk the horizontal engine slowly.
Draw the referral loop back to prospects.
This is the operating system — not motivation.

slides.title: Business Is Built One Cycle at a Time
slides.body: What happens if we repeat this every week?
slides.speakerNotes:
Use the circular diagram.
Emphasize weekly cadence — compounding beats intensity.
Connect referrals back to the top of the funnel.
discussionQuestions: What would change in 4 weeks if you ran this engine every week?

slides.title: Build Your Business Engine — Weekly & Monthly
slides.body:
Use your numbers. Build your engine. Make it repeatable.

Complete the SPIKE Business Engine Canvas™ in the Playbook — weekly targets and monthly projections.
slides.speakerNotes:
Launch the interactive Business Engine Canvas.
Monthly = Weekly × 4 unless they override.
Allow 30–40 minutes for Page 1.
discussionQuestions: Can this engine produce the business you want?

slides.title: Small Activities → Big Businesses
slides.speakerNotes:
Reframe the engine as a freedom machine.
Systems create activities; activities create clients; clients create revenue; revenue creates freedom.

slides.title: The Business Engine — One Cycle. Endless Growth.
slides.body:
Build it. Run it. Repeat it.

10 Prospects · 5 Discovery · 3 Presentations · 1 Client · ₱10,000 · 3 Referrals
Repeat the cycle every week. Compound the results.
slides.speakerNotes:
Deep-dive each step with definitions from the slide.
Use a squad example: if prospects go from 10 to 20, what happens downstream?
Preview Page 2 Growth Simulation.

slides.title: Refine. Repeat. Grow.
slides.body:
The best businesses are built through continuous improvement.

Refine your numbers · Repeat the cycle every week · Grow your venture.
slides.speakerNotes:
Page 2 of Business Engine Canvas — levers, growth simulation, Year 1 commitment.
Encourage squads to pick ONE lever and model the impact.
discussionQuestions: Which lever will create the biggest improvement for your squad?

slides.title: Your Venture Now Has an Engine
slides.body:
Client Experience → Winning Strategy → Business Engine → Growth

Yesterday: understand clients.
Today: build a business.
Tomorrow: scale your advisory venture.
slides.speakerNotes:
Squad share — 3–5 minutes each.
Close: Do you still have an idea, or do you now have a venture?
Preview Day 14.
discussionQuestions: Do you still have an idea — or do you now have a venture?
